title: NavMesh Boundary Issues
subtitle: AI Navigation
context: Squad-based shooter, 4v4 multiplayer with complex level geometry
problem: 40% of AI agents got stuck on navmesh edges during boundary transitions
Players noticed rubber-banding at map edges
QA flagged as game-breaking due to AI becoming unresponsive
solution: Implemented NavLinkProxies with custom movement component override
Overrode MoveRequestAccepted to add edge prediction logic
Added debug visualization for navmesh borders in development builds
Created editor tool to auto-generate links at level design time
impact: Eliminated 15% of QA tickets related to navigation
Approach documented and reused across 5 maps

title: Network Replication Spam
subtitle: Multiplayer Performance
context: 32-player cooperative game with hundreds of interactive objects
problem: Server network utilization spiking to 95% at 20+ players
Increased packet loss and lag spikes
High bandwidth costs on dedicated servers
New players experiencing initial freeze on join
solution: Implemented relevancy system with distance-based prioritization
Changed always-replicate properties to conditional with DOREPLIFETIME
Created custom compression for player state structs
Added async loading for non-critical replicated actors
impact: Network traffic reduced by 62% per player
Server tick rate stabilized at 30Hz with 32 players

title: Async Save Memory Accumulation
subtitle: Memory Management
context: Save system for RPG with 200+ persistent actors
problem: Memory usage growing by ~100MB every save operation
Eventually causing crashes on lower-end hardware
Save operations taking longer over time (up to 30s)
Garbage collection not reclaiming save-related objects
solution: Identified circular references preventing GC in save pipeline
Fixed TArray allocations not being cleared after async write
Added explicit object table cleanup after each save cycle
Implemented save batching to reduce overhead
Added memory profiling to detect future leaks early
code: TArray<uint8> SavedData; /* Cleanup added after save */
impact: Save time consistent at 1.2s regardless of save count
